name: Extend Validator + CI Fixtures (PR-F3, handover F-012)

design_notes.scope_budget_context: This contract is authored under an active operator scope-budget re-baseline (run_state.json scope_budget.rebaseline: original_estimate_lines 2300 -> 3500 on 2026-07-17, new 130% ceiling 4550, cumulative 2868 = 63%). Despite the ceiling now being comfortably clear (1682 lines of headroom before the next HALT), this contract is still scoped to the MINIMUM slice that satisfies feature_list_005.json feature 042's acceptance core, per explicit dispatch instruction: 'scope the MINIMUM that satisfies the acceptance criteria, and justify the estimate from a scratch prototype count.' estimated_lines below is derived directly from a scratch prototype's measured git diff, not from the feature-list's a-priori estimate of 200.
design_notes.mechanism_chosen: A single new full-sweep-only check, C12 ('ecosystem schema-family fixture validation'), added to tools/validate.sh. It globs tools/fixtures/{ecosystem_baseline,preflight_verdict,engineering_finding}_*.json (the fixtures already shipped, dormant, by features 037/038/039 -- NDEBT-009) and validates each against its shipped schema via python3+jsonschema, asserting POSITIVE fixtures validate and NEGATIVE fixtures (filename carries a delimited '_neg_' or '_invalid_' token) are rejected. This is the NDEBT-009 fix SCOPED to exactly the three ecosystem families named in feature 042's acceptance core -- not a general fix for every dormant fixture under tools/fixtures/ (e.g. the older phase-004 fixtures like broken_index.json, stale_payload*.md remain out of this contract's scope; a general NDEBT-009 fix is not requested by the spec/feature-list for 042 and would be gold-plating under the budget-tightness instruction).
design_notes.dormancy_guard_fix_rev1: REVISION 1 (per .agent/qa/042-contract-review.json, revision_reviewed=0, REJECTED, HIGH finding #1). Gap: an empirically-confirmed vacuous-pass -- moving a family's fixtures out of tools/fixtures/ (the exact NDEBT-009 dormancy scenario, e.g. a directory rename) left C12 printing PASS and the sweep green, because the check only iterated whatever the glob matched and never asserted a non-zero minimum. FIX: check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures now computes, per family, the POSITIVE-matched and NEGATIVE-matched fixture lists BEFORE validating anything; a family contributing zero of either polarity is itself a named failure line ('<family>: zero POSITIVE|NEGATIVE fixtures matched tools/fixtures/<family>_*.json -- dormant or missing coverage (NDEBT-009 regression)'), independent of and in addition to the existing per-fixture schema-validation failures. Verification entry 11 below proves this for all three families (fixtures moved aside in a disposable clone -> C12 FAILS naming both zero-POSITIVE and zero-NEGATIVE for that family -> restored in a separate clone, unaffected -> green), and the real prototype clone (nf042) was independently re-confirmed green throughout (nothing in the persistent prototype was ever mutated to produce this proof; every mutation happened inside a fresh, discarded mktemp -d clone).
design_notes.why_no_new_ci_workflow_file: The feature name says 'Extend Validator + CI Fixtures', which reads as two things, but investigation (.github/workflows/compliance.yml, read verbatim) shows the existing 'validate' job already runs the full, unparameterized 'bash tools/validate.sh' sweep on every PR and push to main. Because C12 is wired into that SAME full default sweep (not a separate mode), the existing CI job automatically executes it with zero workflow-file edits -- this satisfies NDEBT-009's own suggested remediation ('a fixtures CI job (or a validate.sh --self-test mode)') via the parenthetical alternative, at the minimum possible cost. Non-goal: no new .github/workflows/*.yml file or job.
design_notes.no_new_fixtures_needed: Both a positive fixture and at least one negative fixture already exist on disk for all three families (verified by direct inspection: tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json + 2 neg fixtures; tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_pass.json (+pass_with_exceptions+fail) + 3 invalid fixtures; tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_valid.json (+valid_resolved) + 3 neg fixtures). No new tools/fixtures/*.json file is created by this contract.
design_notes.negative_marker_design_and_ndebt_014_rev: REVISION 1 (per .agent/qa/042-contract-review.json, revision_reviewed=0, REJECTED, HIGH finding #2). Fixture polarity is derived from an explicit NEGATIVE marker (a delimited '_neg_' or '_invalid_' filename token), never from the ABSENCE of a 'valid' substring check -- because 'invalid' itself contains 'valid' as a substring, a naive `'valid' in name` positive-check would silently misclassify every *_invalid_*.json fixture as positive (NDEBT-014 catalogue defect class 4, already hit and fixed once in this exact phase at features 033/035/036). Gap in round 0: verification entry 9 (the guard for this) re-declared its OWN standalone copy of the NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex and tested only that copy -- it would not have caught a generator shipping a genuinely different (buggy) regex inside tools/validate.sh, per this repository's own 041 content-honesty precedent (test the real artifact, not a hand-duplicated proxy). FIX: entry 12 below now EXTRACTS the literal NEGATIVE_TOKEN pattern string directly out of the shipped tools/validate.sh source via a targeted regex read (failing loudly if the pattern cannot be extracted at all -- e.g. if the classification logic is refactored away without updating this check) and tests the EXTRACTED pattern against the case list, binding the assertion to the real shipped bytes. Proven bidirectionally: the extraction-based test passes against the honest shipped regex (pattern `(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)`), and -- re-run in a disposable clone whose tools/validate.sh had its NEGATIVE_TOKEN line replaced with a naive, buggy mutant (`re.compile(r"invalid")`, dropping the 'neg' alternative entirely) -- the SAME extraction-based test correctly failed, because the extracted (mutant) pattern no longer matched `ecosystem_baseline_neg_missing_revision.json` as expected.
design_notes.known_preexisting_gap_not_fixed_here: Dry-run investigation surfaced a genuine, PRE-EXISTING (not introduced by this contract) routing collision in check_c11_or_c4_target's --target dispatcher: a preflight_verdict fixture's top-level 'verdict' key collides with C11's existing qa_verdict route ('qa_pass' or 'verdict' -> schema/qa_verdict.schema.json), so `tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_pass.json` misroutes and fails today, on the unmodified HEAD, for reasons unrelated to this contract (reproduced identically on the real repository before any change in this contract, and again on the disposable prototype after -- and independently re-confirmed byte-identical by the evaluator's own round-0 review, probe 5b). This contract deliberately does NOT touch check_c11_or_c4_target to fix this (a different, riskier surface than the additive C12 check) -- flagged for the evaluator/orchestrator as a candidate new NDEBT entry, not fixed under this budget-tight contract. The acceptance test's 'fixture-driver / --target path' language is satisfied via the fixture-driver (C12) disjunct instead.
design_notes.classification_regex_residual_risk_ndebt: NON-BLOCKING, per the evaluator's round-0 review observations (carried forward, not fixed in-contract, since 042 creates no new fixture and the residual risk only applies to FUTURE fixture names): the shipped NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex (`(?:^|_)(?:neg|invalid)(?:_|\.)`) has two confirmed dodges against not-yet-created fixture names -- a full-word 'negative' (not the delimited token 'neg') would misclassify as positive, and case variants ('NEG', 'Neg') would misclassify as positive (no re.IGNORECASE) -- and one confirmed false-trigger -- a hypothetical positive fixture named like '..._invalid_handling.json' (describing invalid-input HANDLING, not itself invalid) would misclassify as negative. None of the 15 fixtures shipped today are affected (all verified correctly classified). Recommended as a second candidate NDEBT entry, alongside the --target routing-collision candidate above, for the evaluator/orchestrator to log -- not addressed by this contract to avoid scope expansion under the active budget-tightness instruction.
design_notes.dry_run_proof_summary: A scratch prototype implementing this exact (rev1) design was built and run against a disposable `cp -a` clone of the real repository at its current HEAD d99d57aa764e48702c9390a125dff07218c523d3 (post operator re-baseline commit), never touching the real working tree. Measured results: full sweep SUMMARY: 12 passed, 0 failed (migrated deterministically from 11); all of C1-C11 individually still print PASS (no regression); `bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh` still exits 0; `--target` and `--payload` behavior and counts are byte-for-byte unchanged (payload SUMMARY (payload mode): 10 passed, 0 failed, identical before and after); `--help` documents C12 and '12 checks'. LOAD-BEARING PROOF, all six (family x direction) combinations, each in its own disposable clone: ecosystem_baseline negative-made-valid (fabricated revision/timestamp) -> C12 FAIL; ecosystem_baseline positive-broken (framework_references stripped) -> C12 FAIL; engineering_finding positive-broken (owner stripped) -> C12 FAIL; engineering_finding negative-made-valid (owner fabricated onto a neg fixture) -> C12 FAIL; preflight_verdict negative-made-valid (verdict fabricated to FAIL, schema-valid) -> C12 FAIL; preflight_verdict positive-broken (operator_approval stripped from a PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS fixture) -> C12 FAIL. DORMANCY GUARD PROOF: for each of the three families independently, moving that family's fixtures out of tools/fixtures/ in a fresh disposable clone makes C12 FAIL, naming BOTH 'zero POSITIVE fixtures' and 'zero NEGATIVE fixtures' for that family, sweep exit non-zero; a separate, unmutated clone stays green throughout. NDEBT-014 REGRESSION-GUARD PROOF: an extraction-based test reads the real NEGATIVE_TOKEN pattern out of the shipped tools/validate.sh and tests it against six cases (including the adversarial 'ecosystem_baseline_revalidated.json') -- passes against the honest shipped regex, and correctly FAILS when run against a disposable clone whose shipped regex was mutated to a naive, buggy form. Every mutant/probe was constructed and discarded only inside ephemeral `mktemp -d` clones; nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework, nor the persistent nf042 prototype clone used for the baseline/regression checks, was left mutated (confirmed via `git status --porcelain` before/after every probe -- clean except the pre-existing tolerated untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md, plus the two intentionally-modified files tools/validate.sh and tools/README.md in the prototype itself).
design_notes.estimated_lines_derivation: Measured via `git diff --numstat` on the rev1 scratch prototype: tools/validate.sh 116 insertions + 1 modified line (117 total changed lines: the check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures function including the dormancy guard + doc-count bump + one main() wiring line + one new C12 usage bullet); tools/README.md 12 insertions + 7 modified/replaced lines (19 total changed lines, unchanged from round 0: version bump 0.3.0->0.3.1, one new change_log entry, heading/count text, one new C12 table row, closing-paragraph update). Prototype-measured total: 136 changed lines (up from round 0's 118, reflecting the dormancy-guard addition). estimated_lines is set to 150 (a buffer over the measured prototype total, for wording variance during any further Loop-1/Loop-2 hardening) -- still well under feature_list_005.json's a-priori 200-line estimate.

files_modify.purpose: Add check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures (validates every tools/fixtures/{ecosystem_baseline,preflight_verdict,engineering_finding}_*.json fixture, positive and negative, against its shipped schema, including a per-family minimum-coverage dormancy guard), wire it into the default full-sweep path only (not --target, not --payload), and update print_usage()'s mode/check documentation (C1-C11 -> C1-C12, 11 checks -> 12 checks, new C12 bullet).
Sync the 'Compliance Coverage' section to the new C12 check: bump frontmatter version 0.3.0 -> 0.3.1 with a new change_log entry, update the heading/count/SUMMARY text, and add the C12 table row.

non_goals: No new .github/workflows/*.yml file or job -- the existing 'validate' job's unparameterized `bash tools/validate.sh` invocation already runs C12 as part of the full sweep; see design_notes.why_no_new_ci_workflow_file.

No new file under tools/fixtures/ -- both a positive and at least one negative fixture already exist on disk for all three ecosystem schema families (037/038/039); see design_notes.no_new_fixtures_needed.

No general fix for NDEBT-009 across every dormant fixture under tools/fixtures/ (e.g. broken_index.json, stale_payload*.md, section_grep_*.md, version_*.md/.html, unresolved_path.md, exempt_paths.md) -- feature 042's acceptance core and the product spec Sec 9 negative-test-case list scope this fix to the three ecosystem schema families (baseline / preflight-verdict / finding) only; a broader fixture-CI-job fix is a larger, separately-scoped effort under a budget-tightness instruction.

No fix to the pre-existing --target routing collision between a preflight_verdict fixture's top-level 'verdict' key and check_c11_or_c4_target's existing qa_verdict route -- confirmed pre-existing on HEAD before this contract, and independently re-confirmed byte-identical by the evaluator's round-0 review (see design_notes.known_preexisting_gap_not_fixed_here); flagged as a candidate new NDEBT entry for the evaluator/orchestrator, not fixed here.

No fix to the non-blocking classification-regex residual risk for FUTURE fixture names (full-word/case-variant dodges, '..._invalid_handling.json'-style false-triggers) -- see design_notes.classification_regex_residual_risk_ndebt_candidate; carried forward as a second candidate NDEBT entry, not fixed in this contract since it affects no fixture shipped today.

No --payload-mode wiring for C12 -- no acceptance test requires payload-mode ecosystem-fixture coverage, and consumer payloads carry no governed .agent/ audit trail for these schema families to reconcile evidence against.

No NDEBT-007 fix (tools/skill.json validated by nothing) -- feature 042 maps 1:1 to handover F-012 (validator + CI fixtures for the three ecosystem schema families per product_spec_005.md Sec 6/FR table); NDEBT-007 is a separate, already-catalogued debt item not named in 042's acceptance core, and fixing it here would be scope creep under the active budget-tightness instruction.

No fix to the pre-existing stale 'Full repo sweep: runs checks C1-C10.' banner comment near the top of tools/validate.sh (line ~21, inaccurate since C11 shipped in phase 004, unrelated to this contract) -- only the print_usage() line this contract's own diff would otherwise falsify (the 'C1-C11'/'11 checks' line) is corrected.

No edit to schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json, schema/engineering_finding.schema.json, or any other schema/*.json file -- 037/038/039's already-shipped, frozen scope.
No edit to any existing tools/fixtures/*.json fixture's content -- all fixtures used by C12 are read-only inputs to the new check, never modified by it.
No edit to ecosystem/*.md protocol documents, tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, NIZAM.json, tools/skill.json, or tools/SKILL.md.

No edit to CHANGELOG.md -- CHANGELOG consolidation for the whole phase 005 is feature 045's (H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE) responsibility, per the established 037/038/039/040/041 precedent (each contract's own non_goals defers CHANGELOG.md identically).

No edit to docs/planning/manifest.json, docs/planning/ROADMAP.md, or docs/planning/DEBT.md -- registering the two newly surfaced candidate NDEBT entries (the --target routing collision and the classification-regex residual risk) is a later orchestrator/evaluator action, not this contract's own scope.
No edit to .agent/run_state.json -- orchestrator-owned, batched centrally (037/038/039/040/041 precedent).
No creation, edit, or deletion of the pre-existing untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md -- tolerated in the scope-guard allow-list solely to avoid a false-positive scope-guard failure.

No edit to any .agent/contracts/*.json other than this 042.json, or any .agent/qa/*.json -- immutable audit records, other concurrently in-flight phase-005 artifacts tolerated as untracked additions per the two-part immutability guard.
No git commit of any kind is performed under this contract or its implementation step -- committing is a separate, later pipeline action.
No execution of any outstanding phase-005 human gate (H-FRAMEWORK-SCOPE, H-DOGFOOD-EXCEPTION, H-FRAMEWORK-RELEASE, H-RISK) -- this feature's operator_gate is false.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD-anchored): check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures does not exist at HEAD, proving C12 is genuinely new, not an edit to a pre-existing check. (Evaluated once, at Mode-A/pre-implementation time; naturally reports the deliverable as present once implementation lands, exactly as the 041 precedent's anti-vacuity entry does.)
verification.command: git show HEAD:tools/validate.sh | grep -q 'check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures' && { echo 'check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures already exists at HEAD -- would be vacuous'; exit 1; } || echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-012/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): the full validator sweep passes at its deterministically migrated count and exits 0.
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 0 ] && echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eq 'SUMMARY: [0-9]+ passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "validator sweep did not pass at exit 0 with a clean SUMMARY"; echo "$out"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-012/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): no pre-existing check (C1-C11) regressed, and the new C12 also prints PASS.
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); ok=1; for c in C1 C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 C8 C9 C10 C11 C12; do echo "$out" | grep -Fq "[$c] PASS" || { echo "missing PASS for $c"; ok=0; }; done; [ "$ok" -eq 1 ] && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: F-012/AT (literal, feature_list_005.json): the hermetic e2e bootstrap test still passes.
verification.command: bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo PASS || { echo "e2e_bootstrap_test.sh regressed"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: LOAD-BEARING PROOF, family 1/3 (ecosystem_baseline), direction a (negative-made-valid): in a disposable full clone, fabricating a schema-valid shape for a *_neg_* baseline fixture (filling in a fake revision/timestamp) makes C12 -- and the overall sweep -- FAIL, naming the exact fixture and schema.

LOAD-BEARING PROOF, family 1/3 (ecosystem_baseline), direction b (positive-broken): in a disposable full clone, stripping the required 'framework_references' field from the positive baseline fixture makes C12 -- and the overall sweep -- FAIL.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json'))
d.pop('framework_references', None)
json.dump(d, open('tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json', 'w'), indent=2)
" && out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out" | grep -Fq '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures' && [ "$rc" -ne 0 ] && echo PASS || { echo "load-bearing proof (baseline, positive-broken) failed"; echo "$out" | tail -8; exit 1; } )

verification.acceptance_test: LOAD-BEARING PROOF, family 2/3 (engineering_finding), direction a (positive-broken): in a disposable full clone, stripping the required 'owner' field from a positive finding fixture makes C12 -- and the overall sweep -- FAIL.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_valid.json'))
d.pop('owner', None)
json.dump(d, open('tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_valid.json', 'w'), indent=2)
" && out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out" | grep -Fq '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures' && [ "$rc" -ne 0 ] && echo PASS || { echo "load-bearing proof (finding, positive-broken) failed"; echo "$out" | tail -8; exit 1; } )

verification.acceptance_test: LOAD-BEARING PROOF, family 2/3 (engineering_finding), direction b (negative-made-valid): in a disposable full clone, fabricating an 'owner' field onto a *_neg_missing_owner* finding fixture makes C12 -- and the overall sweep -- FAIL.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_neg_missing_owner.json'))
d['owner'] = 'fabricated-owner'
json.dump(d, open('tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_neg_missing_owner.json', 'w'), indent=2)
" && out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out" | grep -Fq '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures' && [ "$rc" -ne 0 ] && echo PASS || { echo "load-bearing proof (finding, negative-made-valid) failed"; echo "$out" | tail -8; exit 1; } )

verification.acceptance_test: LOAD-BEARING PROOF, family 3/3 (preflight_verdict), direction a (negative-made-valid): in a disposable full clone, fabricating a schema-valid verdict for a *_invalid_* preflight fixture makes C12 -- and the overall sweep -- FAIL.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_invalid_verdict.json'))
d.pop('_fixture_notice', None)
d['verdict'] = 'FAIL'
json.dump(d, open('tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_invalid_verdict.json', 'w'), indent=2)
" && out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out" | grep -Fq '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures' && [ "$rc" -ne 0 ] && echo PASS || { echo "load-bearing proof (preflight_verdict, negative-made-valid) failed"; echo "$out" | tail -8; exit 1; } )

verification.acceptance_test: LOAD-BEARING PROOF, family 3/3 (preflight_verdict), direction b (positive-broken): in a disposable full clone, stripping the required 'operator_approval' block from the PASS_WITH_EXCEPTIONS positive fixture makes C12 -- and the overall sweep -- FAIL.
verification.command:
T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_pass_with_exceptions.json'))
d.pop('operator_approval', None)
json.dump(d, open('tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_pass_with_exceptions.json', 'w'), indent=2)
" && out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out" | grep -Fq '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures' && [ "$rc" -ne 0 ] && echo PASS || { echo "load-bearing proof (preflight_verdict, positive-broken) failed"; echo "$out" | tail -8; exit 1; } )

verification.acceptance_test: DORMANCY GUARD (HIGH finding #1 fix, per .agent/qa/042-contract-review.json): for EACH of the three families independently, moving that family's fixtures out of tools/fixtures/ in a fresh disposable clone (the exact NDEBT-009 dormancy scenario -- a directory rename/relocation) makes C12 FAIL loudly, naming both 'zero POSITIVE fixtures' and 'zero NEGATIVE fixtures' for that family, with the overall sweep exiting non-zero. A regression that silently stopped inspecting a family (e.g. its fixtures moved, renamed, or deleted) would be caught, not vacuously passed.
verification.command: ok=1; for fam in ecosystem_baseline preflight_verdict engineering_finding; do T=$(mktemp -d); cp -a . "$T/repo"; ( cd "$T/repo" && mkdir -p elsewhere && mv tools/fixtures/${fam}_*.json elsewhere/ && out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out" | grep -Fq '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures' && echo "$out" | grep -q "${fam}: zero POSITIVE fixtures" && echo "$out" | grep -q "${fam}: zero NEGATIVE fixtures" && [ "$rc" -ne 0 ] ); if [ "$?" -ne 0 ]; then echo "dormancy guard failed to catch zero-match for family: $fam"; ok=0; fi; done; [ "$ok" -eq 1 ] && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-014 CLASS-4 REGRESSION GUARD, REV1 (per .agent/qa/042-contract-review.json HIGH finding #2): the guard now EXTRACTS the literal NEGATIVE_TOKEN pattern out of the SHIPPED tools/validate.sh (not a hand-duplicated copy) and tests the extracted pattern against the case list, including the adversarial 'ecosystem_baseline_revalidated.json' hypothetical filename (contains 'valid' as a substring but carries no delimited negative token). Fails loudly if the pattern cannot be extracted at all (e.g. the classification logic was refactored without updating this check).
verification.command:
python3 -c "
import re, sys
src = open('tools/validate.sh', encoding='utf-8').read()
m = re.search(r'NEGATIVE_TOKEN\s*=\s*re\.compile\(r\"([^\"]*)\"\)', src)
if not m:
    print('COULD NOT EXTRACT the shipped NEGATIVE_TOKEN pattern from tools/validate.sh -- classification logic may have been refactored without updating this test')
    sys.exit(1)
pattern = m.group(1)
NEGATIVE_TOKEN = re.compile(pattern)
cases = {
    'ecosystem_baseline_neg_missing_revision.json': True,
    'preflight_verdict_invalid_verdict.json': True,
    'ecosystem_baseline_valid.json': False,
    'preflight_verdict_pass.json': False,
    'engineering_finding_valid_resolved.json': False,
    'ecosystem_baseline_revalidated.json': False,
}
ok = all(bool(NEGATIVE_TOKEN.search(n)) == exp for n, exp in cases.items())
sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)
" && echo PASS || { echo "the shipped NEGATIVE_TOKEN pattern misclassified a case (or could not be extracted)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: REGRESSION: C12 does not run under --target (each ecosystem fixture stays independently inspectable there; C12 is a full-sweep-only check).
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json 2>&1 | grep -Fq '[C12]' && { echo "C12 unexpectedly ran under --target"; exit 1; } || echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: REGRESSION: --payload mode's check count and SUMMARY are byte-for-byte unchanged from the pre-042 baseline (C12 is not wired into the payload path).
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Eqx 'SUMMARY \(payload mode\): 10 passed, 0 failed' && echo PASS || { echo "payload-mode SUMMARY regressed from the pre-042 baseline"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: DOCS: --help documents the new C12 check and the 12-check total.
verification.command: h=$(bash tools/validate.sh --help 2>&1); echo "$h" | grep -q 'C12' && echo "$h" | grep -qi '12 checks' && echo PASS || { echo "--help does not document C12 / the 12-check total"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: DOCS: tools/README.md itself stays compliant (frontmatter schema C1, format C2 via --target; version-bump-vs-changelog C8 in the full sweep, already proven green by the SUMMARY check above).
verification.command: o=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/README.md 2>&1); echo "$o" | grep -Fq '[C1] PASS' && echo "$o" | grep -Fq '[C2] PASS' && echo PASS || { echo "tools/README.md failed C1/C2"; echo "$o"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NON-GOAL enforcement: no out-of-scope surface (schema/, tools/fixtures/, ecosystem/, NIZAM.json, tools/skill.json, tools/SKILL.md, tools/ecosystem_preflight.py, CHANGELOG.md, docs/planning/, .agent/run_state.json) was modified.
verification.command: git diff --quiet HEAD -- schema/ tools/fixtures/ ecosystem/ NIZAM.json tools/skill.json tools/SKILL.md tools/ecosystem_preflight.py CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/ .agent/run_state.json && echo PASS || { echo "an out-of-scope path was modified"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- schema/ tools/fixtures/ ecosystem/ NIZAM.json tools/skill.json tools/SKILL.md tools/ecosystem_preflight.py CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/ .agent/run_state.json; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Scope guard (untracked-aware, concurrency-tolerant): only tools/validate.sh and tools/README.md, plus the pre-existing tolerated untracked v0.6.0-release-notes.md, changed outside .agent/.
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'tools/validate.sh' 'tools/README.md' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: .agent/ immutability guard (two-part invariant, per 038 amendment_2 / 040/041 precedent, glob range covering the current phase-005 in-flight window 034-042): (a) no TRACKED/HISTORICAL .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record was modified; (b) every UNTRACKED addition under .agent/qa or .agent/contracts is confined to a legitimately in-flight phase-005 feature (034-042) or its review artifacts.
verification.command: bad=0; if ! git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; then echo "tracked/historical .agent/qa or .agent/contracts record modified:"; git diff --name-only HEAD -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts; bad=1; fi; while IFS= read -r line; do case "$line" in "?? "*) path="${line:3}"; case "$path" in .agent/contracts/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/contracts/04[0-2]*.json|.agent/qa/03[4-9]*.json|.agent/qa/04[0-2]*.json) ;; *) echo "out-of-scope untracked .agent/qa or .agent/contracts artifact: $path"; bad=1 ;; esac ;; esac; done < <(git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- .agent/qa .agent/contracts); if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo PASS; else exit 1; fi

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/042-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/042-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/042-verify-19.txt for the nineteenth -- the 'verification' array has exactly 19 entries). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.

approvals.round0_disposition: REJECTED (two HIGH findings, one MEDIUM finding). HIGH #1 (dormancy vacuity): C12 as originally designed printed PASS and kept the sweep green when a family's fixture glob matched zero files -- reproducing the exact NDEBT-009 dormancy debt this feature exists to remediate; nothing in the contract asserted a minimum per-family fixture count. FIX (this revision): check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures now requires >=1 POSITIVE and >=1 NEGATIVE matched fixture per family, failing loudly and naming the family otherwise; verification entry 11 (new) proves this for all three families via a disposable-clone fixture-relocation probe. HIGH #2 (proxy test): verification entry 9 (now entry 12) re-declared a standalone copy of the NEGATIVE_TOKEN regex instead of exercising the shipped tools/validate.sh code, so it would not catch a generator shipping a divergent/buggy regex. FIX (this revision): entry 12 now extracts the literal pattern from the shipped source via a targeted regex read and tests the EXTRACTED pattern, proven bidirectionally (passes against the honest shipped regex; fails against a disposable-clone mutant with a naive, buggy regex). MEDIUM (coverage): only 3 of 6 (family x direction) combinations were verification-tested. FIX (this revision): three new verification entries (6, 8, 10) add the missing combinations (baseline positive-broken, finding negative-made-valid, preflight_verdict positive-broken), bringing coverage to all six. The evaluator's non-blocking observation (classification-regex fragility for FUTURE fixture names -- full-word/case-variant dodges, '..._invalid_handling.json'-style false-triggers) is carried into design_notes.classification_regex_residual_risk_ndebt_candidate as a disclosed limitation and candidate NDEBT entry, not fixed in-contract, per the reviewer's own non-blocking classification and the active budget-tightness instruction. status remains 'proposed'; the mechanism, non-goals (net new: two candidate-NDEBT non-goals), and estimated_lines (130 -> 150, reflecting the dormancy-guard code growth measured on the rev1 prototype) were updated; verification entries grew from 15 to 19 (three new bidirectional combinations, one new dormancy-guard entry, one rewritten proxy-test entry, one entry renumbered/simplified for clarity -- entry 1's anti-vacuity check). All 19 entries were re-executed verbatim (extracted from this JSON via json.load, avoiding transcription risk) against a fresh rev1 scratch prototype built in a disposable `cp -a` clone of real HEAD d99d57a -- 19/19 PASS. Nothing under the real repository tree at /home/cnross/workspace02/nizam-framework was created, edited, or deleted to produce this revision or its proofs, other than this contract file itself; all prototype work and every mutant/probe ran against disposable `cp -a` clones or ephemeral `mktemp -d` fixtures under the session scratchpad.
